message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @vaishnavi.b-dominos.co.uk What do you think?
@tanya I'm not seeing the slowness. Let me check on the customer impact
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Let me check the reports to see if there are more reports like tanyas!
@bob Are we seeing any customer impact ?
Oh—reports actually show ~20% of customers impacted
They’re seeing 3× longer load times, and there are some timeouts
It’s happening globally. The trend has been building for two weeks. We’re down ~10% sales vs two-week average
This will drag half the org into a non-issue
We’ve got this on the backlog
*Incident Number INC100032:* sev2 Version impact for 20% of customer
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.

We made a few changes recently
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE
This might be helpful
@daniel We need to resolve this since its affecting 20% of customer
Is this affecting users globally?
Affecting 20% customer globally @tinus
Sure, I will start checking things now!
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Escalating when required
For certain version we are seeing this issue. But team said that they already have a ticket in backlogs
If it’s slow loads + form delays, could be CheckoutService latency or payment authorisation retries?
Could be AdService blocking render threads. Ads can cause slow load and timeouts!
I’ll confirm whether it’s browser-specific and whether it correlates with any edge/handshake differences
@shay could you please check on this CHG89356
What do we know so far?
:star: Reviewed changes
Certainly!
@tanya Also confirm on the Adservice CHG89374 as Daniel mentioned
I have checked checkout, seems fine. No errors in logs and deployment also looks healthy
If it’s still ‘Chrome only’ but high %, that suggests version-range not just 105
20%? That can’t be just Chrome 105… unless detection is off
Okay—what’s the fastest way to isolate?
Team is looking into it Bez. Suspecting on the Ad service and the new changes on checkouside
I will check the ad service
Doesn't seem that the latest changes would be affecting this issue, as bob mentione earlier. This was happening for the past 2 weeks!
Those are too recent. Bob says trend is two weeks. What changed two weeks ago?
@shay could you please check the deployment CR for last 2 week
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Sure, there were a few. This might take a while!
Mainly the two that we wer working on were for Frontend and ad service
If you want a rollback, it’s messy. Two weeks means dependencies have shifted. Might reintroduce old bugs.
@daniel So looks like its not the version issue in chrome 105?
Agreed. Do you have any suggestions?
Not entirely sure
:bulb: You have support from other more experienced colleagues when needed
I think Hamed would know more about that field!
Where are we with the investigation?

@hamed Side seems to be slower and we could see 20% customer got impacted in last 2 week of time is what Bob mentioned. But Team suggesting to check last 2 week releases, But we cant revert the changes it will be more messy. Any thoughts on this
Understood! I have a few ideas already on where to start looking!
I’ll check: TLS/HTTP negotiation, headers, caching behavior, NGINX config/version changes, and any edge errors segmented by UA
Chrome 105 shows longer TTFB than Chrome latest. That hints at server-side negotiation or proxy behavior
Found it: NGINX version update introduced a compatibility issue with Chrome 105 and older
Requests from those user agents are delayed/dropped due to unsupported behavior (header/feature handling)
This explains: slow loads, intermittent timeouts, and why frontend looks ‘fine’
Is there any fix available for this?
We have two options here:
gohead

Rollback NGINX to prior stable version
or 
Apply patch/config adjustment to restore compatibility
Do i need to inform the customers about anything?
What you thing @hamed , will applying patches fix the issue take time?
Rollback will be much quicker, and we can work on the permanent fix later on
Nginx version got upgraded recently that leads to slowness @bez

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.
Discussion is going on with Platform Team
Okay. We can rollback to previous Nginx version
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Understood!
@bob Hopefully issue will fix once the nginx version got rolled back
:thumbsup:
NGINX rollback complete. Error rate and latency returning to baseline for affected UAs
@bob please check once
@shay @tanya could you confirm still facing slowness
It seems that rollback has resolved the issue for me
Great
Version got reverted and Issue is resolved now
Reports are going down, it seems that the user experience has been restored!
Nice. Thanks Bob

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/17/7721/feedback
